zh.Staunton, Nottinghamshire, , England: 英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史道頓
zh.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham於1275年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史道頓。他和Cecily DeLoudham至少有1個女兒。他去世於1344年在英國英格蘭艾可維坦享年69歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Loudham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Kirkton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from any of numerous places in Scotland so called, named as ‘church settlement’.

en.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham was born in 1275, in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Cecily DeLoudham. He died in 1344, in Witham, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham est né en 1275, à Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Cecily DeLoudham. Il est décédé en 1344, à Witham, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham wurde 1275 in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Cecily DeLoudham mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1344 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Witham, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham è nato il nel 1275 nel Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Cecily DeLoudham. Morì nel 1344, nel Witham, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudhamは1275年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアスタウントンで生まれた。彼はCecily DeLoudhamとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1344年にイギリスイギリスエセックスウィサムで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham(은)는 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에서 태어났다. 그는 Cecily DeLoudham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1344년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 위섬에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham родился в 1275 г. в местности стаунтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Cecily DeLoudham. Он умер в 1344 г. в местности витам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham nació en 1275, en Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Cecily DeLoudham. Murió en 1344, en Witham, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 69 años.
